friends and commands excellent food and drink. He is also
very mirthful. When the Rasi indicated by the decanate is
strong and is aspected by its lord, the result will be as stated
above.
The development or otherwise of the different limbs of a child
is deduced from the nature of the decanates of the 12 bhavas
beginning with the Lagna. The first decanates of the twelve
houses represent the head, eye, ear, nostril, cheek, jaw, and
mouth on the light side and the same on the left too. Note that
the second and twelfth houses stand for the right and left eye
respectively. Similarly the second decanates of the houses
represent the neck, shoulder, arm, side, heart, chest and navel
on the two sides. The last ones represent likewise pelvis, genital
organ, testicle, thigh, knee, calf and legs, the 12th being anus.
According to some scholars this order should be adhered to only
when the first decanate of a Rasi is rising. Otherwise the order
